item.title: Anti-integrin αvβ6 Immunoglobulin G antibodies are detected in pediatric colonic Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Anti-integrin αvβ6 Immunoglobulin G (anti-αvβ6 IgG) has been identified as a biomarker for ulcerative colitis (UC) in adults, but they were also detected in pediatric Crohn's disease (CD). We hypot...

item.title: Mapping the landscape and future directions of stem cell therapy for inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Stem cell research & therapy - Review - This study systematically analyzed 170 registered clinical trials to clarify the global development landscape of stem cell therapy for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and its core challenges, and ...

item.title: Cross-sectional associations of clinical predictors and physical activity in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Pediatric research - Reduced physical activity (PA) is common in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and may compromise health. This study aimed to examine cross-sectional associations between PA and biomedical,...

item.title: Optimising multidisciplinary team care in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease: a healthcare improvement initiative from a dedicated referral centre.
item.description: BMJ open quality - Paediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) requires complex, multidisciplinary care. However, variation in service delivery and limited insight into patient and family experience may impact care q...

item.title: Surgically Treated Perianal Abscess in Children: Clinical Characteristics and Subsequent Diagnosis of Crohn's Disease.
item.description: European journal of pediatrics - To characterize clinical features and evaluate the proportion of children presenting with perianal abscess who were subsequently diagnosed with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and to explore fact...

item.title: Paediatric autoimmune liver disease in Europe, the prospective ERN R-Liver registry.
item.description: JHEP reports : innovation in hepatology - Most literature on paediatric autoimmune liver disease (p-AILD) comprises single-centre, retrospective studies. This study aims to describe robust, real-world data for p-AILD during the first year ...

item.title: Gut Health Responses to Nutritional Interventions in Paediatric Crohn's Disease, Including the Potential Outcomes of Mucosal Barrier Preservation: A Systematic Review.
item.description: Nutrients - Systematic review - Background/Objectives: Dietary treatment may play a complimentary part alongside established medical treatment pathways for children with Crohn's disease. The aim of this review was to explore the ...

item.title: Fungal colonization and ASCA/p-ANCA positivity in inflammatory bowel disease: a cross-sectional study from Turkey.
item.description: Journal of infection in developing countries - Recent research indicates that individuals with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) exhibit distinct intestinal fungal communities compared with healthy individuals. This study examined the relationsh...
